say.text: There is a place between the ticks of every clock. A purple country where unfinished moments wash ashore. Three men arrive at the same instant, from three different centuries, through three different wounds in the world.
Hana was pulled through the light — you! Suit-wearer! Your city was inside the rift that took her!
My city? My family froze mid-breath because of THAT one — the neon mercenary! This has cyber-nonsense written all over it!
Says the ghost in the grey suit wearing MY face. Both of you are wearing my face. Do you understand how far outside my job description this is?
Your face? That scar over your eye — I EARNED that scar. My mother's killer gave it to me.
No… I got mine the day Kurosawa took me. Same eye. Same scar. That's — that's not possible.
Ministry checkpoint, 2081. Same eye. Same scar. …I don't like this math.
Three shards — vermilion, cobalt, violet — rise from three pockets and begin to HOWL. The suit swings his briefcase first.
Yield! Your stance breaks the same way mine does — low on the left, where the old wound aches. I know it because it is MY wound.
Fighting you feels like… arguing with myself in a mirror. And LOSING.
Cute reunion. My turn. The three-voiced thing under the Ministry sent me here — which makes you two accessories to the worst week of my life.
The third exchange never lands. The sky of the rift-country opens like a lidless eye, and something older than calendars leans down to watch.
OH, DON'T STOP ON MY ACCOUNT. I HAVE WAITED THREE LIFETIMES FOR THIS DINNER THEATER.
YOU STILL DON'T REMEMBER? A STORY, THEN: ONCE, A TRAVELER LEARNED TO WALK BETWEEN THE HOURS. HE FOUND MY LARDER — THE MOMENTS I HARVEST FROM YOUR LITTLE WORLDS — AND HE OBJECTED.
HE FOUGHT ME. TOKIHITO, THE TIMELESS ONE. HE NEARLY WON. SO I DID WHAT ANY CHEF DOES WITH TOUGH MEAT: I CUT HIM IN THREE, WIPED THE MEMORY OFF EACH PIECE, AND SET THEM TO MARINATE IN SUFFERING.
A SON WHO LOSES A MOTHER. A FATHER WHO LOSES HIS NAME. A GUN WHO WILL LOSE HIS ONLY FRIEND. GRIEF IS THE FINEST SEASONING — AND YOU THREE ARE *DONE*.
…The altar in the keep. Three voices.
The numbers that were always the same numbers.
The thing under the Ministry. It called me "splinter".
Three shards ignite. Three scars burn on the same eye. Three hands — calloused by sword, briefcase, and trigger — reach for each other across five hundred years.
For my mother. For Hana.
For Yumi. For Kenta. For every cooked ledger.
For Ren. …For me. For US.
I REMEMBER.
Where three men stood, one figure remains — robes of unspent seconds, hair like dawn, and a blade that remembers every hand that ever held it.
Kuroyami — you fed on my worlds long enough. The Gate of Ash stands at the north of this nowhere. I am coming through it, and the hour between hours is coming OFF the menu.
Beyond the Gate of Ash: a throne of stopped clocks, a floor of swallowed birthdays, and the Demon God of the Hour Between Hours, unbound and enormous.
THE MEAL SEATS ITSELF AT MY TABLE. BOLD. I ATE THE FOURTH CENTURY FOR LESS.

You cursed me into three griefs, demon. You made a boy bury his mother, a father sign away his name, a gunman aim at his friend. Here is what you failed to understand: grief does not tenderize. It TEMPERS.
THEN LET US TEST THE BLADE, LITTLE ETERNITY. COURSE ONE.
The demon god's form cracks like an egg of midnight — and what hatches is WORSE. The clocks on the throne begin to run backwards, screaming.
FINE DINING IS OVER. NOW I EAT THE TABLE, THE CHAIRS, THE RESTAURANT, AND THE CONCEPT OF LUNCH.
The Chrono Edge passes through Kuroyami and out the other side of midnight. For one instant, the demon god looks — relieved.
AH. …SO THAT IS WHAT AN ENDING TASTES LIKE. I ALWAYS WONDERED WHY YOU MORTALS HOARD THEM—
The hour between hours collapses gently, like a tent after a festival. And the Timeless One stands alone with three lifetimes in his hands and one choice left to make.

I could stay whole. Walk the centuries. But there is a sister waiting behind a rift, a family waiting on a frozen street, and an empty table at a bazaar bar. They don't need a god. They need HIM. And him. And him.
So — one soul, three mornings. Kuroyami split me as a punishment. I return as a promise.
VERMILION: the rift over the crossroads closes from the inside, and a ronin walks east under a moving sky, twice as sharp and half as alone.
COBALT: the crane finishes its swing, the commuters take their step, and an accountant walks into the Kurosawa-gumi's world carrying evidence and absolutely no fear.

VIOLET: a merc wakes at his table in the bazaar bar. Across from him, a friend who never missed a drink is just sitting down. "Vex. About that WORLD-real favor—" "Yes," says Vex. "Whatever it is: yes."
Somewhere between the ticks of every clock, there is a quiet purple country where nothing is ever eaten again.
— THE THREADS ARE ONE. —
